text:
GENITOURINARY ONCOLOGY -- FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Cycle 2, Week 6 of Ipilimumab + Nivolumab (Yervoy/Opdivo)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Margaret Okonkwo is a 63-year-old woman with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) with sarcomatoid differentiation presenting today for her cycle 2 ipilimumab/nivolumab infusion and toxicity assessment. She is now approximately 6 weeks into first-line combination immune checkpoint therapy.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okonkwo is well-known to our service. By way of brief background: she was diagnosed with IMDC intermediate-risk metastatic ccRCC involving bilateral pulmonary metastases and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, with a large right renal primary (11.4 cm) and infrarenal IVC thrombus. Pathology demonstrated WHO-ISUP grade 4 disease with approximately 22% sarcomatoid differentiation. Genomic profiling showed VHL and PBRM1 mutations, CDKN2A homozygous deletion, and high PD-L1 expression (CPS ~55). She underwent cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection and IVC thrombectomy approximately 10 weeks ago, which was technically successful with negative surgical margins, though final pathology confirmed 9 of 14 harvested lymph nodes positive for metastatic disease with extranodal extension. A pre-treatment baseline CT performed roughly 6 weeks post-operatively confirmed four bilateral pulmonary metastases (right lower lobe 2.1 cm, right upper lobe 1.2 cm, left lower lobe 0.9 cm, left upper lobe 0.7 cm) as the sole sites of residual measurable disease.

Approximately 8 weeks after surgery, she initiated first-line ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks plus nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks per the CheckMate 214 schema, chosen given her intermediate IMDC risk category, sarcomatoid histology, and high PD-L1 expression. She completed cycle 1 without significant difficulty. Cycle 2 is being administered today.

Since cycle 1, she reports the following symptoms:

Fatigue: She describes a low-level fatigue that began within the first week of therapy and has been consistent since. She characterizes it as feeling "more tired than usual, but manageable." She is still able to perform light household tasks and short walks but has curtailed some of her usual activities. She grades this a 2-3 out of 10 in severity. This is consistent with Grade 1 immune-related or infusion-related fatigue.

Nausea: She reports intermittent mild nausea, predominantly in the first 4-5 days after her infusion. She has been using ondansetron 4 mg orally as needed with good effect. No vomiting. No difficulty maintaining oral intake. This is consistent with Grade 1 nausea, managed.

Bowel changes: She notes she has been having approximately 2 loose stools per day above her personal baseline (normally 1 well-formed stool per day; now 2-3 soft-to-loose stools, without urgency). She denies frank watery diarrhea. No blood or mucus in stool. No nocturnal awakening due to bowel urgency. No significant abdominal cramping. She does not describe this as debilitating. This is consistent with Grade 1 diarrhea. I have discussed with her the importance of monitoring carefully -- the threshold for concern is 4 or more stools above baseline per day, blood in stool, significant abdominal pain, or fever, at which point she should call our triage line immediately.

Skin: No rash. No pruritus. No hypopigmentation or vitiligo-like changes.

Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, stiffness, or swelling.

Respiratory: She denies new or worsening shortness of breath, cough, or chest discomfort. She had baseline mild exertional dyspnea attributed to her pulmonary metastases; this is not worsening.

Endocrine: She denies new symptoms of palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, increased sweating, or new-onset constipation beyond the above-noted bowel changes. No headache, visual changes, or other symptoms to suggest hypophysitis.

Neurological: No new headache, diplopia, facial droop, focal weakness or numbness, or cognitive changes.

Cardiovascular: No chest pain, new palpitations, or peripheral edema.

Hypertension: Her blood pressure at home has been running around 130/80 mmHg per her home log. She is maintained on amlodipine 10 mg/day for pre-existing hypertension.

Pain: She denies significant pain. The post-surgical right flank discomfort has largely resolved. She occasionally uses acetaminophen for minor musculoskeletal aches. No back pain or bone pain.

Appetite: Mildly reduced appetite, particularly around the time of infusion, but maintaining weight and oral intake.

Psychosocial: She reports feeling cautiously optimistic about treatment. Her adult daughter accompanies her today. She has been in good communication with our nursing team via the portal.

She has NOT developed any symptoms consistent with immune-related adverse events requiring systemic corticosteroids at this time.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: Fatigue (Grade 1), nausea (Grade 1, controlled with ondansetron), loose stools 2 above baseline (Grade 1 diarrhea). Mildly reduced appetite.
Negative: No fever, no chills, no significant weight loss. No rash, pruritis, or skin blistering. No arthralgias or myalgias. No dyspnea at rest or new-onset dyspnea. No cough. No pleuritic chest pain. No palpitations. No diplopia, ptosis, or visual changes. No headache. No dysuria. No hematuria. No edema. No new lymphadenopathy perceived by patient. No jaundice. No right upper quadrant pain.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
1. Metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, right kidney, Stage IV (cT3bN2M1) -- see above
2. Post right radical nephrectomy, retroperitoneal lymph node dissection, IVC thrombectomy
3. Hypertension -- on amlodipine 10 mg
4. Hyperlipidemia -- on rosuvastatin
5. Remote tobacco use history: 20 pack-years, quit at age 48

SOCIAL HISTORY
Retired secondary school administrator. Lives with adult daughter who assists with transportation to appointments. No current tobacco or alcohol use. No illicit drug use. Sedentary-to-light activity level currently given treatment-related fatigue. Of West African descent.

FAMILY HISTORY
Not previously documented in detail in our notes. Patient reports a maternal uncle with kidney disease (uncertain if malignant); no known family history of hereditary cancer syndromes. Germline testing from tumor NGS panel showed no pathogenic variants in VHL, SDHA/B/C/D, FH, TSC1/2, MET, or FLCN, making a hereditary RCC syndrome very unlikely.

ALLERGIES
No known drug allergies.

MEDICATIONS
1. Amlodipine 10 mg orally once daily (hypertension)
2. Rosuvastatin 20 mg orally once daily (hyperlipidemia)
3. Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg orally as needed (pain)
4. Ondansetron 4 mg orally every 8 hours as needed (nausea) -- prescribed at cycle 1 start
5. Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (today is cycle 2)
6. Nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (today is cycle 2)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 132/80 mmHg, heart rate 76 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 16 breaths/min, temperature 36.8 C, oxygen saturation 97% on room air, weight 74.2 kg (stable from baseline).
ECOG Performance Status: 1.

General: Ms. Okonkwo is a well-appearing, well-nourished woman in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Conversational and engaged.

Head, Eyes, Ears, Nose, Throat: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Pupils equal, round, reactive to light. Extraocular movements intact. Oropharynx: moist mucous membranes, no oral mucositis, no thrush. No thyroid enlargement or tenderness on palpation.

Neck: Supple. No cervical, supraclavicular, or infraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing, crackles, or rhonchi. Good air entry throughout.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Healed midline laparotomy scar in good condition, without wound dehiscence or signs of infection. No hepatosplenomegaly appreciated. Right flank: well-healed, slightly tender on deep palpation at right renal bed -- expected post-surgical finding, non-concerning. No palpable masses. Bowel sounds normal.

Skin: No rash. No vitiligo. No erythema, blistering, or skin thickening on palms or soles. No jaundice or scleral icterus.

Musculoskeletal: No joint swelling, erythema, or effusions in major joints. Full range of motion of bilateral upper and lower extremities.

Neurological: Alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 in all extremities. Gait steady. No focal deficits.

Lymphadenopathy: No new peripheral lymphadenopathy on examination.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (drawn prior to infusion today)
Complete Blood Count:
  - WBC: 6.8 x10^9/L (normal)
  - Hemoglobin: 10.4 g/dL (mildly low; was 10.8 g/dL at therapy start, slight downtrend -- likely combination of post-surgical anemia not yet fully corrected and treatment-related effect)
  - Hematocrit: 31.2%
  - Platelets: 242 x10^9/L (normal)
  - Differential: within normal limits, no eosinophilia, no lymphopenia

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  - Sodium: 138 mEq/L, Potassium: 4.0 mEq/L, Chloride: 101 mEq/L, CO2: 25 mEq/L
  - BUN: 18 mg/dL, Creatinine: 1.1 mg/dL (stable; eGFR ~58 mL/min/1.73m^2 -- acceptable for single kidney status post-nephrectomy)
  - Glucose: 96 mg/dL
  - Total bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL, Direct bilirubin: 0.2 mg/dL
  - ALT: 28 U/L (normal), AST: 31 U/L (normal), Alkaline phosphatase: 88 U/L (normal)
  - Albumin: 3.9 g/dL
  - Total protein: 7.1 g/dL

Thyroid Function:
  - TSH: 3.2 mIU/L (normal range 0.5-4.5; slightly elevated from baseline of 1.8 mIU/L but well within normal limits -- monitoring trend)

Additional:
  - LDH: 188 U/L (normal)
  - Urinalysis: trace protein (1+), no blood, no WBC
  - Stool occult blood: not collected today (patient denies bloody stool; low clinical suspicion)

Note: LFTs are within normal limits, clearing the threshold for safe ipilimumab administration (no immune hepatitis at this time). Thyroid function stable. Renal function stable at single-kidney baseline. Mild anemia continues to be monitored.

---

IMAGING RESULTS
No new imaging today. The most recent staging CT was the pre-systemic therapy baseline scan performed approximately 6 weeks post-nephrectomy, demonstrating four bilateral pulmonary metastases (largest 2.1 cm in the right lower lobe) as the sole metastatic burden. Restaging imaging is planned at approximately 12 weeks from treatment initiation per protocol. MRI brain performed at staging was negative for intracranial disease.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Okonkwo is a 63-year-old woman with IMDC intermediate-risk metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (sarcomatoid differentiation 22%, high PD-L1 CPS ~55, VHL/PBRM1 co-mutated), currently on first-line ipilimumab plus nivolumab per CheckMate 214 protocol. She is presenting today for cycle 2 administration at week 6.

1. METASTATIC ccRCC -- ACTIVE TREATMENT, CYCLE 2 INDUCTION
   - Disease status: No restaging imaging at this interval; awaiting formal response assessment at approximately 12 weeks. Clinical status is stable -- no new symptoms of disease burden. The mild anemia and fatigue are consistent with ongoing recovery from major surgery and treatment effects rather than disease progression.
   - Current regimen: Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV q3 weeks (INDUCTION, today is dose 2 of 4) + Nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV q3 weeks. She will receive a total of 4 doses of ipilimumab; nivolumab will continue as maintenance at 480 mg IV q4 weeks after the induction phase.
   - TODAY: Ipilimumab and nivolumab to be administered in infusion suite following this visit. Premedications: acetaminophen 650 mg PO and diphenhydramine 25 mg IV per protocol.
   - Rationale for regimen choice is well-documented: sarcomatoid features and high PD-L1 expression are among the strongest predictors of benefit from dual checkpoint blockade in ccRCC, supported by CheckMate 214 subgroup analyses and the Motzer et al. data showing particularly robust overall survival advantage in the sarcomatoid subgroup.

2. IMMUNE-RELATED ADVERSE EVENTS (irAEs) -- MONITORING
   - Grade 1 diarrhea: 2 loose stools per day above baseline. Currently no intervention beyond dietary counseling (low-residue diet recommendation, adequate hydration). Not meeting threshold for colitis management. Patient educated extensively about escalating symptoms -- instructed to call triage if stools increase to 4+ above baseline, develop bloody diarrhea, fever, or significant abdominal pain. Stool cultures to be sent if diarrhea worsens. Hold rules and steroid protocol reviewed with patient.
   - Grade 1 fatigue and nausea: Both are mild, manageable, and expected. Continue ondansetron PRN. Encourage light physical activity as tolerated to combat fatigue. Deferred iron supplementation for now given anemia not severe enough and etiology is multifactorial.
   - Thyroid: TSH 3.2 mIU/L (normal, trending up from 1.8 mIU/L at baseline but not clinically significant). Will recheck TSH with next labs at cycle 3. If TSH rises above 4.5 or patient develops hypothyroid symptoms (fatigue, cold intolerance, weight gain, constipation), will initiate levothyroxine. Checkpoint inhibitor-associated hypothyroidism is common and well-managed with thyroid hormone replacement without need to discontinue immunotherapy.
   - Liver function: ALT/AST normal. Will continue to monitor at each cycle. Hepatitis would require dose holds and potential systemic steroid therapy.
   - No clinical concern for pneumonitis, myocarditis, nephritis, hypophysitis, or adrenal insufficiency at this time.

3. ANEMIA
   - Hemoglobin 10.4 g/dL, mildly reduced from post-surgical baseline of 10.8 g/dL. Likely multifactorial: post-operative recovery (intraoperative blood loss with 2 units pRBC transfused, recovery of erythropoiesis), inflammation from immunotherapy, and potentially mild treatment effect. Iron studies not yet checked; will order today (serum iron, TIBC, ferritin, reticulocyte count) to assess for correctable iron deficiency, particularly given surgical blood loss history. No transfusion threshold reached; patient is asymptomatic from anemia beyond contributing to fatigue. Monitor.

4. HYPERTENSION
   - Blood pressure well-controlled at 132/80 mmHg on amlodipine 10 mg/day. Continue current antihypertensive regimen. Note that VEGFR-targeted therapy (cabozantinib, if considered in the future) carries significant hypertension risk; current immunotherapy has a lower BP-related profile. Continue monitoring.

5. HYPERLIPIDEMIA
   - Continue rosuvastatin. No lipid panel ordered today; due for routine check.

6. RENAL FUNCTION (SINGLE KIDNEY)
   - Creatinine 1.1 mg/dL, eGFR ~58 mL/min -- stable for solitary kidney following nephrectomy. Nephrology is not involved currently. BP control is important for renal protection. Avoiding nephrotoxic agents. Will continue monitoring creatinine each cycle.

7. PATIENT EDUCATION AND GOALS OF CARE
   - Discussed the treatment plan and timeline: 4 induction doses of ipilimumab (2 completed today), then nivolumab maintenance. Formal restaging CT anticipated at approximately week 12.
   - Reviewed irAE warning signs at length. Patient and daughter verbalize understanding and have our triage line number.
   - Patient remains motivated and engaged. ECOG PS 1 -- functional.
   - Supportive care: referral to our dietitian for nutritional support during treatment was offered; patient declined at this time but is open to in the future.

FOLLOW-UP:
- Return in 3 weeks for cycle 3 assessment and infusion (ipilimumab dose 3 + nivolumab dose 3)
- Labs to be drawn prior to visit: CBC, CMP, TSH, LFTs, iron studies
- If any concerning new symptoms (especially diarrhea escalation, rash, dyspnea, confusion, or severe fatigue) develop before next scheduled visit, patient instructed to call our oncology triage line
- Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast planned for approximately 12 weeks post-treatment initiation

Time spent in direct patient care and counseling: approximately 40 minutes.

Electronically signed by: [Attending Genitourinary Oncologist]
